sentences.text: Hey, June.
Sweet.
How's it going?
Alright, how are you guys?
Not too bad, the summer's been flying by, I'm sure it has for you.
Well, we are sending out, you know, November, December renewals, so that's years over.
It's a wrap, huh?
Yep.
Hey, Leslie, hey Sam, it's nice to meet you.
Hey, guys.
Hey, Austin.
Hello.
Am I supposed to be on this call, June?
Sure, I mean, it's totally up to you.

I know… so, other analysts are selected, as a subject matter expert in the… each department, and then, you know, I know operations team, you've been testing out other platforms and other competitors, so it'll be nice for you to kind of see, hey, Tiffany.
Thank you.
You know, what are the other options that are out there in the market?
Perfect!
what did we do, Leslie?
You join, and then you're like, oh, how do I get out of this thing?
No, I just saw a bunch of faces that weren't in my department, so I wanted to make sure I was in the right place.
Sounds good.
Thanks for joining.
Tiffany makes me feel better.
Well, I had two different invites, so I wasn't sure which one to, like.
Yeah.
So I was like, we'll do this one first, then I'll go to the next one.
Yeah, other ones just was a space folder, and it has no link in it, intentionally, so yeah.
Okay.

And then, in Austin, Corey, there are… a lot of folks are right now in the, you know, budget meetings and whatnot that's happening, so… they are, they may or may not join or join late, but, you know, since you're recording, which I was going to request, if you could share the link and the password, so that way I can forward it to them, and, you know.

That way, they could kind of watch the, demo session, and … I'm sure they are pretty… you know, savvy with the things, but, it'll be good for them to kind of see how you walk, you know, walk us through the options and things that's out there, and I'm sure there are other options that are, you know, brand new since, you know, when you guys had a demo session a long time ago, so… Hey, Michael.
Thanks for joining.
Hey, Joe.
Yeah, we'll send out the recording.
We like to just kind of walk through the platform as we provide access for everyone.
To your point, June, I think it's fairly intuitive.

And then for those that can't join, or even the group on the meeting, like, we'll introduce ourselves in a second, but Justin and Austin are always available for quick, kind of, personal walkthrough, or to answer any questions that you have, so….
Sounds good.
Well, great.
And yeah, Michael, so I guess as far as, like, kind of the new folks, Leslie, Tiffany, and Sam, as Corey mentioned, I'm Austin Schleisner, Director of Sales here.

Corey is our SVP, and then we also have Justin, who's my other counterpart as Director of Sales, so… Excited to get to meet you all, and I've been, lucky to get to know quite a few folks at Gables so far.
So, … I guess just as far as your three roles, and I know Michael familiar to Sam, Leslie, and Tiffany, I guess, where do you fit in with Gables?
I guess kind of just to make sure to maximize your guys' time on this call.
Sure, I'm in Austin, Texas, and I am the district manager over four different communities, one being a lease up.
Okay.
I am a regional manager.
I'm based in Dallas, the Dallas-Fort Worth area.
I do cover part of Dallas and Houston.
I have, 7 different assets, ranging from lease-ups to stabilized deals, in both markets.
And I'm on the revenue management side, so I work directly for June.

Terrific, and I'd have to assume that you guys, once we get into the trial, that you oversee probably one or multiple of the properties that are selected for the trial, so, … I sent you all the invites.
If you haven't already gotten them, they should be in your inbox.
So, feel free to have it up and kind of pop around while I go over it.
But, a little bit about ApartmentIQ ourselves, if you have… has anybody heard of us before, if not, totally fine, too.
Can just kind of give you the quick, rundown of what we do, and then kind of get into the trial.
I have not heard… heard of you guys before.
I got the invite.
Same.
Same.
Okay, so as far as what we do is we automate the market survey process.

So, I know it previously it sounded like it was a combination of a manual process by your teams, and then kind of drawing that back with the kind of private data concerns, so… the nice thing with ApartmentIQ, all of our data is updated daily, and it's directly from your property websites.
So it's 100% public published data, totally DOJ compliant there.
And ultimately, you're able to see exactly how your property's performing versus your closest competitors.
So all of our data is updated daily, easy-to-read platform, as well as PDF or Excel.
reports, and those can be scheduled to hit your inbox on any day.
And really what that's led to, a combination of our ease to use, our, you know, 100% public, published data compliant, we're now the preferred vendor of over 35 of the top 50, and 7 of the top 10.
So, … I've already been tracking a number of your, you know, essentially the entire Gables portfolio for a while now, and we're excited to kind of show you what we're all about.
The only thing that I'll add is, we invented this daily automated market survey about 7 years ago.
We've been storing publicly available rents for the last 5 years, so Austin will take you through the platform here shortly and show you how to toggle back in time across virtually any any report.
We've become quite popular, to Austin's point, I think, for 3 primary reasons.
We are the most accurate, so Austin said it, everything you're about to see at the latest represents yesterday's data, so everything's updated every single night.
Everything can be traced all the way down to the unit level, and we've just been doing this longer than anyone else.

So our algorithms are the most sophisticated, our availability data is the most accurate, and all of the performance metrics that you care about, occupancy least, etc, are the most accurate, accordingly.
Thank you, Corey.

Just one thing, these property lists here, guys, are, picked… I picked them, only because you know, there are, you know, different locations, they're in renovation, lease-up, student property, you know, AB1482 rent control property, they're either stabilized fee assets, stabilized REBA assets, so, you know, LRO or REBA, and also there are fee properties that are not on revenue management.

So, it's a, you know, diverse mixture, and then, after, you know, this demo session, I'll probably book another call with all together to divvy up the work and to see, you know, what are the, you know, comparable rents are.
Are they looking pretty good?
What's the unit mix?
What's the, you know, notice pricing looks like?
And also, what's the product mix And, you know, even down to pictures, and, you know, we'll compare some reports to what we have and what we can obtain.

And then, you know, other things that we are looking into, what are the competitor selections they are looking, recommending, and then, you know, controlling the quality of the comp set, and a lot of other things that I'm going to be, asking, to help us to kind of validate, and then kind of confirm on those, data points and the reports and processes.
Perfect.

Thanks, June, and I think what you're… what you see here, obviously, is our application, and Junior use case, or Sam, just so you're aware, like, you can ingest all of this data you're about to see in multiple ways as well.
So, we can provide a really seamless, kind of no-code, HTTP endpoint solution to pull this data into your BI tools.
or any sort of data links, data lakes via API, if you prefer as well.

So, and I kind of encourage the group, as you're looking at this, just think about, maybe even your least sophisticated team members, like, what we've built should be able to accommodate everyone in this Zoom and the least sophisticated team members that you have.
Really, really easy, to use for all team members.
But, Austin, I'll let you kind of take the group through, What they're about to get access to.
Terrific.

So, as Jim mentioned, these were already pre-picked, properties, so you have the 7, my properties, but, you know, if we were lucky enough to partner, obviously everything would be just right here under the gables.
… profile, and you'll have the three sections, Properties, Research, and Explore.
I'll get into all three, but as far as kind of, like, the bread and butter of, you know, automating the market survey, all of that's going to be under here, the My Properties.
So I'll jump into domain here.
In the first market survey that you'll see come on up.
is going to… hopefully it will look, to Corey's point, like a standard old-school market survey.
So you'll see your subject property, which will be highlighted here, in the light purple, versus your competitors.

You'll obviously see we're capturing all those metrics that are important to you, including Google reviews, rent rates, net effective rents, so what we're doing is we're scraping and using AI technology to capture all the information directly from the websites.
We're going a little bit more under the hood to the actual leasing portal section to gather all those unit-level information.

So, that's an… ultimately, as you scroll down, you'll see the calculated metrics, and how we're actually getting those calculated metrics is because we obviously know the entire unit count at the property, and then as those units come on and off every single day, we're just making calculations based off of that.
… So, again, 100% public data.
We're using the primary website as the source of truth, just because we found that to be the most accurate.
So, some of our other competitors out there will, you know, maybe scrape an Apartments.
com, for instance.
We just have found that to be much more inaccurate, because of also the amount of availability that you're able to see.
So, essentially, we're able to see the full amount of units that are currently available at the property.
And that's how we're able to get as granular as, for instance, is showing the amount of applications that you're able to see at that property.
Hey, Austin, I assume that with your current client list that is part of their onboarding, they probably ran some sort of analysis to say the accuracy of this against our own internal metrics?
What kind of are the, … kind of your accuracy… I guess, what were your findings from that, from all your customers, on how accurate your calculated metrics are?
Yeah, it's a good call-out.
So, we run, like you mentioned, continuously audits with our largest customers, so… with our largest customers, over 100,000 units, that delta is consistently under 2%.
So it's, very spot on, about as close to public data as you can get.
Some people even kind of would joke that we have the advertised occupancy, that they could even remove that advertised part, just because of how spotted-on it is.
… No integrations whatsoever, but that's ultimately how we're getting that information.
Yeah, Sam, I'll share that, we have a 200,000 unit portfolio as a partner.
They found our occupancy and lease to be within 0.
5% of actuals on average.
We also have some of the larger REITs as partners who use our data to understand performance before the public earnings calls, and have found our data to be, accurate and reliable as well, so….
Great.
Thanks, guys.
Yeah.

Everything that can be as high-level or granular as you want, so obviously… Sam, Michael, your guys, like, when you're really getting into the weeds, you can click on, so like, hey, what were those 11 applications?
You can click on any sort of metric and get a quick pop-up box and see exactly what was the unit number, floor plan type, were they offering a concession, what was that concession value?
All this is just really clean and easy at your fingertips here.
As you scroll down, you can see the broken out by floor plan.
So I'll also call out here, if you see a blank value in this section, I know sometimes this can be confusing off the bat, that just means that there's not currently any studios available at Addison.
But what you can do is, because we have that over 4 years of information, you can toggle to any time frame if you wanted to go back as well and see the last time that they had those advertised.
I have a question.
On the concessions, is there… how do you calculate the lease terms?
So if they're… if they're advertising, you know, 3 to 12 month leases, how do you… how do you factor that concession on the lease terms, if we don't know what lease term they got?
Justin, you wanna… I'll default to that one.
Yeah, so you're saying in regards to the concession, if they say it's 1 month free off of 15-month terms, or, like, a 14-month lease there?
Yeah, what if they're not, like, sometimes properties, we see that they're… they have, like, 3 to 18 month leases, but they're doing 2 months free.
How do you… sometimes I've seen, like, on other platforms where the concession is really high, or really low, depending on what lease term.
I was just curious how you guys are calculating that.
Yeah, so first off, as far as pricing goes, we're gonna deliver the price based off of the best available, so regardless of terms.
And then, that concession, if it does say, you know, one month free off of 14-month terms, for example, we're still going to apply that for the net effective number.
So, it'll still be baked into there.
What's nice is if a concession's attached to a specific floor plan or a unit, we can actually calculate that concession only to those floor plans or units.

So, we can get as specific as the actual, you know, the concession details are, but yeah, in regards to term, we will, we will show the net effective number based off of, you know, regardless of what the term actually is.
Okay.
So, is that used on a 12-month term, in general?
Best available.
So if they offer a 14-month term and that's the cheapest rate, that's the price that we'll reflect in the tool.
Okay.
Is there a preference for 12-month rate?
I just didn't want it to be skewed if they have, like, a 3-month rate… a 3-month rate, and they're doing 8 weeks free.
I doubt they're doing 8 weeks free on a 3-month lease, but I didn't know… want the data to reflect that.
So was there doing, like, a, I don't know, 200% concession or something?

Right, yeah, and … you'll see here, we have historical trends, and that's actually the reason why we go with the best available raises, because that 3-month term, that 6-month term, that could big-time skew the data, so it seems like the lowest advertised price is what normalizes the information over the long run, so….
Gotcha.
Thank you.
Yep.
Going forward, then, as you scroll down, we also have fees and deposits, so becoming more and more important in your markets, having that information right, right in the app.
That will also be in the reports, too, that I'll show you, but that, you'll have access to that both in-app and in your reporting.
And then community level amenities, in-unit, and community level.
It'll just be a nice check mark next to what amenity they have.
Any questions on the market survey before I go forward?
As far as, like, this group type, I would really say the floor plan report here is the most popular.
It just gives you a nice, easy, digestible report broken out by floor plan, and then it incorporates our 4-plus years.
So, … There's a few other smaller companies out there that maybe have about a year to year and a half of, information.
We can have, obviously, the over 4 years in here.
So you can select, I know, like, 30 days.
You can see how your property's been performing, price per square foot-wise.
With a click of a button.
As you go down, it's then one beds, twos, 3s.
You can see leasing velocity, filter that high to low, low to high, by simply clicking on the metric that you want to filter by.
Same goes for Galleria Domain, for instance.
They're the leader here with 13 leased studios.
What are those studios that they're leasing?
Just click on the metric, and you can see.
So it consistently, you know, were it the small studios, was it the large studios?
It looks like they're having the under 500 square foot, a lot more success there.
How do you determine who the competitors are for each community?
And can we add different comps?
Yeah, good call out.
So, right here, in any report, you'll have the edit comps right in the right-hand corner.
Okay.
And we will just pull in anybody, so we have a standard comp match algorithm, so it's gonna be that 1 to 100 score that you'll see right here.
And it's gonna be based off the distance, your bill, the rent rates, and the property class.
But obviously, you guys know your markets the best.
Simply just add right here by clicking add, or type in the property name address that you wish.
So, effectively, we're tracking, over 40 million units every single day, so especially anybody that you guys would genuinely be comping against is gonna have a website, I'd have to imagine.
So, … As long as they have a website, we'll be able to track them for you.
And Leslie, just so you're aware, we've set up this entire group with admin access, so that functionally means you can edit, add, remove anything within the instance.
We do have four levels of user permissioning.
admin would give you the most access, and then read-only would obviously be the most restricted, and then a couple of levels in between.
But, some of our partners, as an example, would like their site team members to be able to edit comps, some wouldn't, but that'll be totally up to you.
Okay, yeah, I'm in there now, and I see that That's great, thank you.
The last call-out, then, too, on the floor plan, just getting as, again, as granular as you wish.
You can toggle over to floor plan, and then this will show you all the different unit types by floor plan.
So it'll show you your E2s, your E1s, your New York Platinums, for instance, and you can see how those have been trending.

Again, a lot of folks I know like to sort by, like, your square footage in here, or if there's specific income-restricted floor plans, too, to compare that apples to apples, you'll be able to see that.
Again, on both market survey and floor plan.
Canem says enough to get in here and just filter by what's important to you, so you can… obviously, there can be a lot of data in here, or as small of data as you want.
So, like, if you just wanted to hone in on just, you know, 3 specific properties, you can just select those specific properties and nice get a subset comparison.
Same goes for your filters of bedroom size, unit style, some big call-outs in there, even a build-to-rent, if you get into that in some of your markets.
Jumping forward then to the historicals report, this will incorporate that historical data that we have on the market.
So, right now I have the last year selected, so the last 12 months, and I'm gonna change it to month over month.
kind of gives a nice, smooth look at what's been performing at your property.
Your property's a purple line, versus the mean, the median, excuse me, and the min and the max.
So as you scroll down, you can see how you've been trending by rent, by month, versus your competitors.
As well as back to that concession value, so in here, it's smoothed out, it's not, to your guys' point, you know, very high, a lot of peaks and valleys.
You can see what time of the year is it more consistent to offer maybe a larger concession than other times of the year.
Again, concession values.
The one I really like to call out, is leased units by property by month.
So even if it was a slow month for your property, how did we do compared to the rest of my competitors?
You can easily have that data right here at your fingertips.

And it might be helpful for the group just to note that we consider just our definition of a lease, so obviously this model is built on publicly available data, so we do have to build in some assumptions.
When a unit leaves a website, we consider it an application.
When it remains off of the website for 7 days, we consider it a lease.
So we do have that holding period, that allows for just more accurate leasing velocity.
We have some less sophisticated Products in this… in the industry that'll overstate leasing velocity, as an example.
If it comes back on the website, you'll see it as a cancellation or denial in the relisted units category.
I'll send as a follow-up, too, but we have a full help center.

I know there, you know, we have over 35 metrics on that note within this app, but within the Help Center, we have a full summary and FAQ, so we have a full glossary of terms that I'll also can send over, so every single metric has a definition tied to it.
So, if you guys have questions on there, as well as, I'll pop back over.
Any metric you can hover over.
So I'll just jump back to your market survey, and you'll see that there's a definition for any single metric, if you have a question on it.
So again, hopefully you're seeing that this is an easy flowing, should be easy to use.
Obviously, everybody on here is experts, but something that even your leasing agents, community managers can use.
And pick up very quickly.
That's kind of the, … the market survey portion.
Any questions on here that… anything you didn't see that you're assuming to see?
Yes, I have a question.
Can you run a vacancy trend or occupancy trend, Via, like, a market or sub-market, or…?
Yeah, that… great call, Francisco.
That was actually gonna be the next portion that I was just gonna go to, so if you wanted, like, kind of a market overview… Yes.
How are you looking at that right now?
Are you using CoStar?
I'm using CoStar, yeah.
Okay.
So I think you'll be living a little bit more here in research, and experience.
So when you have a market, so this is complete nationwide access.
Basically all you would do in here is type in, so I'll type in Dallas, for instance.
But this can be, as you saw, zip code, neighborhood, market, sub-market.
I'll type in Dallas, and this will give you exactly what you just were asking.
So this will give you a 30-day snapshot by floor plan.
as well as it will give you the historical rent growth for up to 4 years, so again, the most publicly, available data stored in the industry here.
Availability, leasing.
You can toggle this between least exposure and advertised occupancy as well, so you can toggle between what metrics you want to see.
And then on the right-hand side is just the interactive map.
So, again, like, if you wanted to get as specific, you can freehand draw, you can draw a radius.
Like, maybe you just wanted this 5-mile radius in North Dallas.
You can click that, and then within seconds, it will create a new snapshot, a new boundary.
So now we're limiting that down to just this under 500 specific properties.
You can see how the rent growth is very different there.
So, same thing applies to whatever boundary we want.
You also have all the filters.

So, obviously, for, your guys' portfolio type, like, if you wanted anything like over 100 plus units built 2010 or above in the last 20 years or so, you can now even create… filter that down even more.
And how are you guys, Calculating their rent growth.

Are you just looking at, you know, or just aggregating Units, you know, versus You know, where they showed up on the website at one point versus where they were listed later in the, you know, again in the future.
Or what have you.
Yeah, so it's gonna be within that range, so whatever range that we had, so this is gonna be back to, the 1st of 2024, and then it's the median asking rent of when that came off.
Okay, so you're looking at the median asking rate of whatever was listed on the website.
During… between those two periods.
Right.
And then the last that it was available.
So, whatever that median price of when that came off.
If there's a lower availability during that, you know, between those two points, or at one of those points.
I mean, could you… Is it possible that that number could be skewed?
If the availability at one point was… you know, 10 units.
And it was maybe highly, you know, highly skewed towards one-beds.
Or two beds, and at another point in time, it was skewed a different way.
Or is there something to account for that?
Yeah, Michael, we actually have a, like, an enhanced version of this coming out, where you're actually going to be able to look by bed type.
It should solve for that, and then additionally, you'll be able to look at historical rent trends at both the gross and net level, as well as price per foot.
So, yeah, to kind of skew any kind of availability, you'll be able to dive a little bit deeper into the data there.
Okay.
Thank you.
Yeah, there will be some more filters, too, by bedroom type.
So basically, right where these filters are that I applied, you'd also be able to put those in there.
As far as what else is available on here, you'll notice new supply.
So, new construction supplies, it's gonna be into 3 sections here, so under construction, scheduled, and in planning, deliveries by quarter, and distribution by size.
So, anything on here, too, you can hover over the individual pins, and it will tell you what the plans are for that… for that new construction.
Lastly, as you scroll across, you'll have demographics.
So you'll have population, income, Age, household, employment.
All that right here, you can toggle through.
With these pop-down tabs.
A nice thing, too, is this overlays with Google Maps, so it can get much more specific for your demographics.
It's not always at the MSA level.
Once you have a property, too, so if you're getting into taking over a possible new deal, then we also have the research.

So, right in here, you can type in the property name, address, or if it doesn't actually exist yet, it's a new development, you can create what your property's going to look like by the address, unit count.
But essentially what you'll do is you'll pull a new report, and just type in the property name that you were looking for, and then in the second, you can pull a real-time market survey.
So, this will all look exactly the same as if it was a My Property.
As you can see, we're just pulling in that closest, competitors, but at any time, you can hit your edit comps here.
You can add or remove those comps, you can look at it at a floor plan level, everything is exactly the same.
And then you can just go ahead and export to PDF or Excel right in the right-hand side.
And, and I could add… under construction or proposed comps?
You can't add a new supply to this, no.
But this would show you exactly… so if you piece this together, it would show you exactly where that was located on there.
Would you like to, Francisco?
I'm trying to understand the question.
I think we're limited within the market server….
So, yeah, so some… sometimes… when I'm creating a market survey, I also factor in Future inventory for that specific, … Subject property.
And what I'm seeing, CoStar, sometimes you're able to select proposed or under construction properties similar to the property that I'm underwriting.
Does that make sense?

in, and… our system will require the presence of online leasing to pull that property into the set, but I get… would you… would you use, like, a projected… like, a forecasted rent rule for the under construction, or I guess I'm just trying to think how you… or you just have the asset in the market survey, and….
It'll be a projected, yeah.
Okay.
Protected rents, yeah.
Okay.
That's helpful.
… That's not the way the system operates currently, but we'll pass that along, and I think we'll ask this group for feedback throughout the pilot, just so you're aware.
We have 30 engineers working on this product every single day, so we're uniquely positioned to be able to act on your feedback as As well as just receive it, but that's helpful.
We'll follow up with you directly on that request.
Thank you.
And then, just to kind of frame what you're looking at.
the My Properties section, that's where we have this small subset of pilot properties set up for you all.
This is where your entire portfolio would live, should we be fortunate enough to partner.
And then, as Austin said, for research, if you just want to run a quick market survey for any property you don't own or manage, you'll do it here.
Three clicks, you'll generate the market survey.
You want to see market or sub-market comparative performance.
you'll jump to Explorer, so… … as I mentioned earlier, we can set up the user permissions however you wish at some point as well.
You may want the entire team to have access to all three modules, or you may not.
Yeah, so that's right in here.
It's as easy as just going to Manage Users, and then obviously everybody in here is at the admin level, but you can select Anywhere from read-only up to admin.
I guess, June, you mentioned earlier, kind of, like, you guys had looked at a few others.
Is there anything, kind of, like, that we could call out that's… that you're looking at to kind of evaluate here, that you've seen in the past?
How would we automate a weekly survey to be emailed to us?
Yeah, great question.
So right here, if you want it in the PDF or Excel, whatever version that you wish.
I'll just do PDF, for example.
You just would type in, … your email address, L.
Brown, and then if you wanted it, like, weekly on Monday, you just would select that cadence, and then you would hit Schedule Email.
Feel free to email me, too, if you would want whatever cadence.
I can set that up, but again, it's just… export, add your email address, and then what cadence, so, you know, weekly on Monday or Tuesday, and then schedule email.
What time does it normally come on the day we select?
Should be by… Justin, is it 8 AM?
Yeah, I was gonna say, that's a good question.
It's, I guess depending on the time zone, I think it's by 9am, Pacific at the latest.
Awesome.
And then just kind of.
There are the… sorry to interrupt, there are the two options, as Austin noted.
I think the PDF report is generally client-facing, or maybe scheduled for on-site team members.
The Excel report's far more comprehensive, so… the Excel report will essentially kind of export all of the reports that you just saw here.
Your filters, the way you've sorted the reports will also stick through the export.
And that's at the user level, so you can set this up to your own specifications.
There's a very simple button within the interface as well to kind of replicate your format to all of your market surveys.
You don't have to do that.
Over and over again, but as you can see here, the PDF, nice, highly branded, you should feel comfortable sharing this with anyone externally, but more of a higher level summary.
At the property level, the bed type level, as well as, like, a concession summary.
But, but the Excel… contains quite a bit of additional information.
The Excel report actually includes every unit that's been available, or leased within your market as well, which we could show you, too.
Sir, I saw a few others coming off mute, though, as far as the questions.
I think the only question I have, and I kind of asked June, but I'm gonna ask you guys.
So, since I'm kind of on the pilot here to review it, I don't see one of my assets on the list.
Is there a way to get an additional one added so that I can, you know, be in it, be working in it, looking at it, you know.
You can generate your own report, right, on research?
